title: 3 Legal Considerations
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
For convenience, this Chapter contains a summary of the main statutes and regulations that pertain to this Part. The material in this Chapter isnât intended to take the place of the statutes and regulations but rather to provide a summary to assist the financial institution. It is recommended that reference is made to the actual statute or regulation for the full text.
Note:	There is no guarantee of accuracy for the online statutes.
3.2	CPA Rules
The Canadian Payment Association (CPA) develops and implements the rules that apply to the clearing and settlement of different types of payments between its member financial institutions. See the Payments Canada website at
www.payments.ca
for more information on CPA Rules.
CPA Rule A6, Items in Dispute
â Provides the procedures by which a negotiating institution can place an item in dispute, and the means by which the dispute can be resolved.
CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions
â
Includes general rules relating to the exchange of AFT Files, procedures applicable to the processing of AFT transactions, as well as the settlement of obligations arising from the Exchange and Clearing of AFT debits and credits between Direct Clearers. It also contains procedures applicable to exception processing, problem resolution, contingency situations, and escalation procedures in the AFT environment.
CPA Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits
â Outlines the procedures for the Exchange for the purpose of Clearing and Settlement of every Pre-Authorized Debit. There are four categories of PADs (Business PAD, Cash Management PAD, Funds Transfer PAD, and Personal PAD).
CPA Standards 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT File
â Addresses the exchange of financial data by data transmission. It provides the standards for the exchange of financial data on AFT files.
CPA Standards 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files
â Lists acceptable AFT transaction codes and AFT return reason codes.
END
